entries.d: A place where you go to watch films on a big screen
entries.s: We are going to the _____ to see a new film tonight.

entries.d: A building where people go to pray
entries.s: There is an old _____ in the centre of our town.

entries.d: A place where you keep your money safe
entries.s: My mum went to the _____ to get some money.

entries.d: A place where you can buy and eat a meal
entries.s: We ate pizza at a nice _____ near the lake.

entries.d: A place where trains stop so people can get on or off
entries.s: We waited at the _____ for the train to Vienna.

entries.d: A shop where you buy medicine when you are sick
entries.s: I went to the _____ to buy something for my cold.

entries.d: A place where visitors can get maps and information
entries.s: We got a free map from the _____.

entries.d: A shop where you can buy instruments or songs
entries.s: I bought a new guitar at the _____.

entries.d: A place where you send letters and packages
entries.s: I went to the _____ to send a letter to my grandma.

entries.d: A big shop where you buy food and other things
entries.s: We buy milk and bread at the _____ every week.

entries.d: A place where doctors help sick or hurt people
entries.s: My friend broke his arm and went to the _____.

entries.d: A building where police officers work
entries.s: We went to the _____ to report the lost bag.

entries.d: To walk by something without stopping
entries.s: You _____ the church without stopping and then turn left at the next corner.

entries.d: To walk forward without turning
entries.s: _____ and the shop is at the end of the road.

entries.d: To walk from one side of the road to the other
entries.s: Look both ways before you _____ so you don't get hit by a car.

entries.d: To change your direction and go to the left side
entries.s: _____ at the traffic lights and you will see the park.

entries.d: To turn at the second road on your right side
entries.s: Walk down the main road. Go past the first turning, then _____ at the next one.

entries.d: Just past where two streets meet
entries.s: The bakery is just _____, only 20 metres away. You turn left and you will see it.

entries.d: All the way to a certain place
entries.s: Walk _____ the church and then stop. Don't go past it.

entries.d: On the other side, facing something
entries.s: The school is _____ the supermarket. They face each other across the road.

entries.d: Very close to something, right beside it
entries.s: My house is right _____ the bakery. The wall is shared.

entries.d: To go from one side to the other
entries.s: We _____ the bridge to get to the other side of the river.

entries.d: A picture that shows where places are
entries.s: We used a _____ to find the way to the hotel.

entries.d: The one after the first; also a very short time
entries.s: Don't take the first street. Take the _____ street on the right, just after the bakery.

entries.d: To keep walking forward in the same direction
entries.s: Don't turn left or right here. Just _____ until you see the park.

entries.d: A place where planes take off and land
entries.s: We went to the _____ very early to catch our plane.

entries.d: To get off one train and get on another
entries.s: We had to _____ in Linz to get to Salzburg.

entries.d: Almost always, nearly every time
entries.s: _____ I walk to school, but sometimes I take the bus.

entries.d: A small bag in your clothes where you put things
entries.s: He put his hands in his _____ because they were cold.

entries.d: Not fast, taking a long time
entries.s: This bus is very _____. We will be late for school.

entries.d: In a place, but you do not know exactly where
entries.s: I left my pen _____ but I cannot find it.

entries.d: A train that goes under the ground in a big city
entries.s: Many people take the _____ to get to work in big cities.

entries.d: An open space in a town where people sell things
entries.s: Every Saturday there is a big market on the _____.

entries.d: In an easy and clear way
entries.s: It's very easy to find. _____ follow the road and you will see it.

entries.d: Words you say or write about something
entries.s: She wrote a nice _____ under the photo to say she liked it.

entries.d: To say or write what you think about something
entries.s: Many people _____ on her photos online, writing 'so pretty!' and 'amazing!' under them.

entries.d: When someone tells you what they think about your work
entries.s: The teacher gave us helpful _____ on our project, telling us what to improve.

entries.d: A person who comes to your house or a place
entries.s: We had a special _____ at school today. A police officer talked to us.

entries.d: To say you will give something to someone
entries.s: My neighbour _____ to help us move the table, even though we did not ask her.

entries.d: When a new shop or place starts for the first time
entries.s: There is a big _____ of a new ice cream shop today!

entries.d: Good and helpful, not bad
entries.s: The teacher always says _____ things about my work, like 'well done' and 'great job'.

entries.d: When someone writes what they think about a film, book, or place
entries.s: I read a good _____ of the new film online, written by a film critic with a 5-star rating.

entries.d: The most bad, the opposite of the best
entries.s: That was _____ meal I have ever had. I hated it.

entries.d: To make someone a bit angry because you take their time
entries.s: Sorry to _____ you when you are busy, but can you help me with this?

entries.d: A place on the road where you wait for the bus
entries.s: I waited at the _____ for ten minutes in the rain.

entries.d: Water that goes up into the air in a public place
entries.s: There is a beautiful _____ in the middle of the square.

entries.d: To start talking when someone else is speaking
entries.s: Please don't _____ me when I am talking. Wait until I finish my sentence.

entries.d: In a nice and respectful way
entries.s: She said 'please' and asked _____ if she could sit down.

entries.d: Red, yellow, and green lights that tell cars when to stop or go
entries.s: Wait for the green _____ before you cross the road.
